ac:rich-text-body.p: Science verification is the programme to validate telescope performance before transitioning to full science operations.

p: Science Verification (SV) is a critical phase in the SKA Observatory's journey to full science operations. During this phase, we validate that the SKA telescopes — SKA-Low and SKA-Mid — are performing as designed and are ready to deliver world-class science.
SV involves commissioning activities that demonstrate:

ul.li: End-to-end system performance across all signal processing pipelines
Data quality and integrity from antenna to archived products
Calibration and imaging performance across the full frequency range
Readiness of Science Data Processor (SDP) pipelines
Archive infrastructure and data discovery capabilities

p: Science Verification maps directly to Array Assembly 2 (AA2) in the SKA construction and commissioning roadmap:

ul.li: Array Assembly 1 (AA1): Basic commissioning and system integration
Array Assembly 2 (AA2): Science Verification — full telescope performance validation
Transition to Operations: Handover to ongoing science operations

p: AA2 represents a fully functional SKA telescope ready to support open community science.

ul.li: SKA-Low Science Verification data: Expected 2027
SKA-Mid Science Verification data: Expected 2029

p: SV datasets will be released progressively as they become available. Early releases may be targeted at specific science cases or frequency ranges as the telescopes come online.
SV data will be made available to the global astronomy community through the SKA Archive. These releases will include:

ul.li: Fully calibrated Observatory Data Products (ODPs) — images, spectral-line cubes, visibility data
Associated metadata and quality metrics
Pipeline processing information for reproducibility

p: The SKA will offer early access opportunities for community researchers to:

ul.li: Test analysis pipelines on real SKA data
Validate science use cases with actual telescope performance
Provide feedback to help improve data processing and archive services

p: SV data enables the broader community to develop and test science analysis pipelines, validation tools, and value-added data product algorithms before Cycle 0 operations begin.

Upon completion of Science Verification and successful performance validation, the SKA will transition to Cycle 0 — the first open call for science proposals. This transition marks the beginning of regular, sustained science operations.

ul.li: The observatory is ready to support diverse science cases
Data products are of known quality and reproducibility
Archive and data discovery services are robust and scalable
The global community is prepared to conduct breakthrough science with SKA data

ac:rich-text-body.p: See Introduction to Observatory Data Products and Advanced Data Products for details on the data types produced during and after Science Verification.
